message:
fix: Return correct search mode in worker error handler

The search worker's error handler was returning a hardcoded mode value
of 'text' when catching exceptions, but 'text' is not a valid SearchMode
type. The valid SearchMode union is:
  'sequence' | 'motif' | 'gene' | 'feature' | 'position'

This type mismatch would cause TypeScript errors at compile time (if strict
type checking caught it) or runtime issues when consumers expected a valid
SearchMode but received an invalid string.

Root cause: The error handler was written with a placeholder mode value
that was never updated to use the actual request mode.

Fix: Changed `mode: 'text'` to `mode: request.mode` to preserve the
original search mode from the request, ensuring type safety and correct
error response structure.

Impact: Search errors now return properly typed responses that match
the SearchResponse interface, allowing consumers to handle errors
without type mismatches.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
